cases.passage: Riya's friend, Aisha, had recently joined a new social media platform, 'EzyConnect'. She was excited to connect with her friends and family, but soon realized that her private messages were being shared with strangers. Aisha had unknowingly downloaded a malicious app that was hacking into her account. She felt embarrassed and concerned about her online safety. She shared her experience with Riya, who advised her to report the incident to the platform's moderators and change her password immediately. Riya also suggested that Aisha enable two-factor authentication and be cautious while downloading apps from the platform's store.

questions.q: What should Aisha do to protect her online identity?
opts: (A) Ignore the incident and continue using the platform.
(B) Report the incident to the platform's moderators and change her password.
(C) Enable two-factor authentication and download more apps.
(D) Delete her account and stop using social media.
questions.explanation: Aisha should report the incident to the platform's moderators and change her password immediately to protect her online identity.

questions.q: Why did Aisha's private messages get shared with strangers?
opts: (A) Because she had too many friends on the platform.
(B) Because she had downloaded a malicious app.
(C) Because she had not enabled two-factor authentication.
(D) Because she had not changed her password in a long time.
questions.explanation: Aisha's private messages got shared with strangers because she had unknowingly downloaded a malicious app that was hacking into her account.

opts: (A) A type of malicious software that hacks into accounts.
(B) An extra layer of security that requires a second form of verification.
(C) A feature that allows users to download more apps.
(D) A service that helps users recover their passwords.
questions.explanation: Two-factor authentication is an extra layer of security that requires a second form of verification, such as a code sent to a user's phone or a biometric scan.

questions.q: Why is it essential to be cautious while downloading apps from a platform's store?
opts: (A) Because it may slow down the device.
(B) Because it may consume more data.
(C) Because it may contain malicious software.
(D) Because it may not have any features.
questions.explanation: It is essential to be cautious while downloading apps from a platform's store because it may contain malicious software that can hack into a user's account.

questions.q: What should users do when they encounter online safety issues?
opts: (A) Ignore the issue and continue using the platform.
(B) Report the incident to the platform's moderators and change their password.
(C) Enable two-factor authentication and download more apps.
(D) Delete their account and stop using social media.
questions.explanation: Users should report the incident to the platform's moderators and change their password immediately when they encounter online safety issues.

cases.passage: During her internship at a tech firm, Nalini was asked to create a strong password for a project. She was advised to include a combination of uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters. However, she found it challenging to remember the password. Nalini's colleague, Rohan, suggested that she use a password manager to generate and store unique passwords for each account. Nalini was hesitant at first but eventually decided to try it out. She was impressed by how easily the password manager generated complex passwords and stored them securely.

questions.q: Why is it essential to include a combination of characters in a password?
opts: (A) To make it easier to remember.
(B) To make it more secure.
(C) To make it more attractive.
(D) To make it more complex.
questions.explanation: Including a combination of characters in a password makes it more secure, as it is harder for hackers to guess or crack.

opts: (A) A tool that generates weak passwords.
(B) A tool that stores passwords securely.
(C) A feature that allows users to download more apps.
(D) A service that helps users recover their passwords.
questions.explanation: A password manager is a tool that generates and stores unique, complex passwords for each account securely.

questions.q: Why did Nalini find it challenging to remember her password?
opts: (A) Because she had too many accounts.
(B) Because she had created a weak password.
(C) Because she had created a strong password.
(D) Because she was not paying attention.
questions.explanation: Nalini found it challenging to remember her password because she had created a weak password that was easy to remember but not secure.

questions.q: What are the benefits of using a password manager?
opts: (A) It allows users to generate weak passwords.
(B) It stores passwords securely and generates complex passwords.
(C) It helps users recover their passwords.
(D) It makes passwords more attractive.
questions.explanation: The benefits of using a password manager include storing passwords securely and generating complex passwords that are harder to crack.

questions.q: Why is it essential to use a unique password for each account?
opts: (A) To make it easier to remember all passwords.
(B) To make it more secure.
(C) To make it more complex.
(D) To make it more attractive.
questions.explanation: Using a unique password for each account makes it more secure, as a hacker who gains access to one account cannot access other accounts with the same password.
